title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · History Workshop
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule for maintenance, commit graphs, index acceleration, and bundles. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G17 is a workshop in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, apply Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety without its stated preconditions, omit the measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety for measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule.
State the Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process. This does not resolve commit to the mission model.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-bundle-backup-verification-v2xtqi" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Bundle Backup Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-bundle-backup-verification-v2xtqi
# scenario-key: trace-v2xtqi probe-iqtx2v boundary-xtqiv2 owner-v2xtqix fault-v2xtqiz
# before-ref: main=a11cev2 candidate=b00b1qi
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning
git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack
# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11cev2 candidate=c0ffeei
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. Competency probe: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A successfully created bundle necessarily contains every ref, reflog, hook, config, LFS object, and worktree needed for recovery.

stimulus.title: Gita Bundle Backup Verification failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A successfully created bundle necessarily contains every ref, reflog, hook, config, LFS object, and worktree needed for recovery.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.

options.text: Keep the plausible Gita Bundle Backup Verification output and hide the violated measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Bundle Backup Verification without its stated preconditions, omit the measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Bundle Backup Verification for measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Bundle Backup Verification condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Gita Bundle Backup Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G26 is a workshop in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. Competency probe: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Bundle Backup Verification through the concrete measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.
